seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Users experiencing 'Selected Model is at Capacity' errors; status updates available via OpenAI incident page, indicating scaling challenges in production AI coding tools.
Upcoming models claim parity with Fable performance at >2x lower cost, faster inference, and reduced censorship, highlighting rapid iteration in frontier model efficiency and accessibility.

global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Decentralized per-agent memory with reuse/exploration pools prevents convergence in multi-agent systems; achieves O(log T) regret, up to 23.8% better accuracy and 49% fewer tokens vs centralized memory.

Graph-based evolving memory frameworks (heterogeneous graphs, Cue-Tag-Content with active reconstruction) for agents; SOTA on LoCoMo/Mind2Web/GAIA and up to 23% gains on long-horizon benchmarks with lower token costs.

Complementary layers for agent systems: n8n for visual workflow orchestration and tool integrations (700+ connectors); LangGraph for stateful, cyclical multi-agent reasoning with persistence and observability.
Open-source AI engineering stack overview emphasizing agent orchestration (LangGraph default substrate), retrieval, serving splits (prefill/decode), and fine-tuning trade-offs for production systems.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Curated list of multi-agent frameworks and durable engines including Temporal, Hatchet, AgentRQ alongside LangGraph/CrewAI for human-in-loop and parallel orchestration.
near_misses.reason: Useful directory but lacks novel technical details or benchmarks from the past 24h.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Daily rank highlights agent skills (NVIDIA/SkillSpector), context compression (headroom), and domain models; emphasizes reusable skills, security, and lower context costs.
near_misses.reason: Aggregates trends but no deep technical explanation of innovations.

notes: Phase 1 yielded limited high-signal technical posts from the exact seed experts (mostly operational updates). Phase 2 semantic/keyword searches surfaced strong memory and orchestration research. Selected only posts with clear technical implications; capped at 6 per main array.
